description:
The actionlib stack provides a standardized interface for
    interfacing with preemptable tasks. Examples of this include moving
    the base to a target location, performing a laser scan and returning
    the resulting point cloud, detecting the handle of a door, etc.
authors: Eitan Marder-Eppstein, Vijay Pradeep, Mikael Arguedas

description:
This package provides a set of simple math utilities to work
        with angles. The utilities cover simple things like
        normalizing an angle and conversion between degrees and
        radians. But even if you're trying to calculate things like
        the shortest angular distance between two joint space
        positions of your robot, but the joint motion is constrained
        by joint limits, this package is what you need. The code in
        this package is stable and well tested. There are no plans for
        major changes in the near future.
ArbotiX Drivers
Extends the arbotix_python package with a number of more sophisticated ROS wrappers for common devices.
Firmware source code for ArbotiX ROS bindings.
Messages and Services definitions for the ArbotiX.
Bindings and low-level controllers for ArbotiX-powered robots.
Extends the arbotix_node package with a number of more sophisticated ROS wrappers for common devices.

description:
A bond allows two processes, A and B, to know when the other has
    terminated, either cleanly or by crashing.  The bond remains
    connected until it is either broken explicitly or until a
    heartbeat times out.

A bond allows two processes, A and B, to know when the other has
    terminated, either cleanly or by crashing. The bond remains
    connected until it is either broken explicitly or until a
    heartbeat times out.

C++ implementation of bond, a mechanism for checking when
    another process has terminated.

Python implementation of bond, a mechanism for checking when
    another process has terminated.

The State Machine Compiler (SMC) from http://smc.sourceforge.net/
    converts a language-independent description of a state machine
    into the source code to support that state machine.

    This package contains the libraries that a compiled state machine
    depends on, but it does not contain the compiler itself.
Contains tests for [[bond]], including tests for [[bondpy]] and [[bondcpp]].

Provides a toolchain running through the robot calibration process. This
     involves capturing calibration data, estimating parameters, and
     then updating the URDF.

Runs an optimization to estimate the a robot's kinematic parameters. This package is a
    generic rewrite of pr2_calibration_estimation.

This package contains a collection of launch files that can be helpful in configuring
    the calibration stack to run on your robot.

This package defines messages for storing calibration samples
     to be used in full robot calibration procedures. This package
     is still unstable. Expect the messages to change.

This package contains a script to generate calibration launch and configurationfiles for your robot.
    which is based on Michael Ferguson's calibration code

Provide a node that extracts checkerboard corners from ROS images.
    This package is still experimental and unstable.
    Expect its APIs to change.

Tools for calculating the intersection of interval messages coming
    in on several topics. This package is experimental and unstable.
    Expect its APIs to change.

Provides a node that reports how long a subset of joints has been
     settled. That is, it calculates how long a set of joints has remained
     within a specified threshold. This package is experimental and unstable.
     Expect its APIs to change.

Extracts checkerboard corners from a dense laser snapshot.
     This package is experimental and unstable. Expect its APIs to change.

Listens on a ImageFeatures topic, and waits for the data to settle.
     This package is experimental and unstable.
     Expect its APIs to change.

Defines helper functions and routines that greatly help when trying to create a settler
    for a specific sensor channel. This package is experimental and unstable.
    Expect its APIs to change.

description: The class_loader package is a ROS-independent package for loading plugins during runtime and the foundation of the higher level ROS "pluginlib" library. class_loader utilizes the host operating system's runtime loader to open runtime libraries (e.g. .so/.dll files), introspect the library for exported plugin classes, and allows users to instantiate objects of said exported classes without the explicit declaration (i.e. header file) for those classes.

description:
This package contains a C++ parser for the Collada robot
    description format. The parser reads a Collada XML robot
    description, and creates a C++ URDF model. Although it is possible
    to directly use this parser when working with Collada robot
    descriptions, the preferred user API is found in the urdf package.
authors: Rosen Diankov, Kei Okada, Ioan Sucan, Jackie Kay

description:
This package contains a tool to convert Unified Robot Description Format (URDF) documents into COLLAborative Design Activity (COLLADA) documents.

    Implements robot-specific COLLADA extensions as defined by
    http://openrave.programmingvision.com/index.php/Started:COLLADA
authors: Tim Field, Rosen Diankov, Ioan Sucan, Jackie Kay

The diagnostic_analysis package can convert a log of diagnostics data
    into a series of CSV files. Robot logs are recorded with rosbag, and
    can be processed offline using the scripts in this package.

diagnostic_updater contains tools for easily updating diagnostics. it is commonly used in device drivers to keep track of the status of output topics, device status, etc.

description:
A framework for writing drivers that helps with runtime reconfiguration, diagnostics and self-test.

    This package is deprecated.

The driver_common stack contains classes and tools that are useful
    throughout the driver stacks. It currently contains:

    driver_base: A base class for sensors to provide a consistent state machine
    (retries, error handling, etc.) and interface

    timestamp_tools: Classes to help timestamp hardware events

This package is currently for internal use only. Its API may change
    without warning in the future.  This package is deprecated.

This unary stack contains the dynamic_reconfigure package which provides a means to change
    node parameters at any time without having to restart the node.

Embeds the TCLAP library inside the ecl. This is a very convenient
     command line parser in templatised c++.

Introduces a compile time concept checking mechanism that can be used
     most commonly to check for required functionality when passing
     template arguments.

The containers included here are intended to extend the stl containers.
    In all cases, these implementations are designed to implement
    c++ conveniences and safety where speed is not sacrificed.

    Also includes techniques for memory debugging of common problems such
    as buffer overruns.

Some fast/convenient type converters, mostly for char strings or strings.
     These are not really fully fleshed out, alot of them could use the addition for
     the whole range of fundamental types (e.g. all integers, not just int, unsigned int).

     They will come as the need arises.

A set of tools and interfaces extending the capabilities of c++ to
    provide a lightweight, consistent interface with a focus for control
    programming.

This includes a suite of programs demo'ing various aspects of the
     ecl_core. It also includes various benchmarking and utility programs for
     use primarily with embedded systems.
Provides an extensible and standardised framework for input-output devices.
This provides an Eigen implementation for ecl's linear algebra.

Template based exceptions - these are simple and practical
     and avoid the proliferation of exception types. Although not
     syntatactically ideal, it is convenient and eminently practical.
Cross platform filesystem utilities (until c++11 makes its way in).

The formatters here simply format various input types to a specified
   text format. They can be used with most streaming types (including both
   ecl and stl streams).

Any tools relating to mathematical geometry.
     Primarily featuring polynomials and interpolations.

Interprocess mechanisms vary greatly across platforms - sysv, posix, win32, there
  are more than a few. This package provides an infrastructure to allow for developing
  cross platform c++ wrappers around the lower level c api's that handle these
  mechanisms. These make it not only easier to utilise such mechanisms, but allow it
  to be done consistently across platforms.
Ecl frontend to a linear matrix package (currently eigen).

Deploys various manipulation algorithms, currently just
    feedforward filters (interpolations).

This package provides simple support to cmath, filling in holes
    or redefining in a c++ formulation where desirable.

Contains transforms (e.g. differential drive inverse kinematics)
    for the various types of mobile robot platforms.

Metaprogramming tools move alot of runtime calculations to be shifted to
    compile time. This has only very elementary structures at this stage.

Provides a signal/slot mechanism (in the same vein as qt sigslots,
     boost::signals etc for intra-process communication. These include
     some improvements - they do not need a preprocessor, are fully type safe,
     allow for simple connections via a posix style string identifier
     and are multithread-safe.
Common statistical structures and algorithms for control systems.

These are lightweight text streaming classes that connect to standardised
     ecl type devices.

This package provides the c++ extensions for a variety of threaded
     programming tools. These are usually different on different
     platforms, so the architecture for a cross-platform framework
     is also implemented.

Timing utilities are very dependent on the system api provided for their use.
	This package provides a means for handling different timing models. Current support

	- posix rt : complete.
	- macosx : posix timers only, missing absolute timers.
	- win : none.
Extends c++ type traits and implements a few more to boot.
Includes various supporting tools and utilities for c++ programming.

These tools inspect and describe your system with macros, types
     and functions.
Color codes for ansii consoles.

These are a very simple version of some of the functions in ecl_converters
     suitable for firmware development. That is, there is no use of new,
     templates or exceptions.

This library provides lean and mean error mechanisms.
    It includes c style error functions as well as a few
    useful macros. For higher level mechanisms,
    refer to ecl_exceptions.

Most implementations (windows, posix, ...) have slightly different api for
     low level input-output functions. These are gathered here and re-represented
     with a cross platform set of functions.
Libraries and utilities for embedded and low-level linux development.

This avoids use of dynamic storage (malloc/new) and thread safety (mutexes) to
     provide a very simple sigslots implementation that can be used for *very*
     embedded development.

Provides a portable set of time functions that are especially useful for
     porting other code or being wrapped by higher level c++ classes.

Contains transforms (e.g. differential drive inverse kinematics)
    for the various types of mobile robot platforms.

This stack aims to bring the common tools and algorithms needed to
    develop navigation algorithms, in particular slam. It does not focus on
    the end-point solution, rather the tools needed to create a variety of
    end-point solutions.

Collection of cmake/make build tools primarily for ecl development itself, but also
     contains a few cmake modules useful outside of the ecl.

Maintains the ecl licenses and also provides an install
     target for deploying licenses with the ecl libraries.
Tools and utilities for ecl development.

This package provides a set of typedef's that allow
  using Eigen datatypes in STL containers

This metapackage depends on the SMACH library and ROS SMACH integration
    packages.

SMACH is a task-level architecture for rapidly creating complex robot
    behavior. At its core, SMACH is a ROS-independent Python library to build
    hierarchical state machines. SMACH is a new library that takes advantage of
    very old concepts in order to quickly create robust robot behavior with
    maintainable and modular code.

this package contains a set of messages that are used by the introspection
    interfaces for smach.

The smach_ros package contains extensions for the SMACH library to
    integrate it tightly with ROS.  For example, SMACH-ROS can call
    ROS services, listen to ROS topics, and integrate
    with
This metapackage depends on the SMACH visualization tools.

description:
The smach viewer is a GUI that shows the state of hierarchical

    values of user data that is passed around between states. The
    smach viewer uses the SMACH debugging interface based on
    the

description: FCL is a collision checking library

Collision detection: detecting models overlap, optionally, triangles overlap. Distance computation: computing minimum distance pair models, distance closest pair points. description:
This library provides a standardized interface for processing data as a sequence 
    of filters.  This package contains a base class upon which to build specific implementations
    as well as an interface which dynamically loads filters based on runtime parameters.

The configuration node loads a given launch configuration and offers services to 
     list or start the contained nodes. It provides additional description 
     extracted from launch file. This is used by node_manager_fkie.

Discover the running ROS Masters in local network. The 
     discovering is done by sending an echo heartbeat messages to a defined 
     multicast group.
     The alternative is to use a zeroconf/avahi daemon to register the ROS 
     master as service and discover other ROS masters.

Synchronize the local ROS master to the remote masters 
     discovered by master_discovery_fkie node. The registration
     of topics and services is only perform by local ROS master.

The metapackage to combine the nodes required to establish and manage a multimaster network. 
    This requires no or minimal configuration. The changes are automatically detected and synchronized.
The messages required by multimaster packages.

Graphical interface, written in PySide, to manage the running and 
     configured ROS nodes on different hosts. For discovering 
     the running ROS master master_discovery node will be used.

tf is a package that lets the user keep track of multiple coordinate
frames over time. tf maintains the relationship between coordinate
frames in a tree structure buffered in time, and lets the user
transform points, vectors, etc between any two coordinate frames at
any desired point in time.
authors: Tully Foote, Eitan Marder-Eppstein, Wim Meeussen

description:
This package contains a set of conversion functions to convert
common tf datatypes (point, vector, pose, etc) into semantically
identical datatypes used by other libraries. The conversion functions
make it easier for users of the transform library (tf) to work with
the datatype of their choice. Currently this package has support for
the Kinematics and Dynamics Library (KDL) and the Eigen matrix
library. This package is stable, and will get integrated into tf in
the next major release cycle (see roadmap).

SVM existing model file, predicted center square good grasping point. good grasping points coordinates direction approach vectors published. DOWNLOAD CODE git HAF_GRASPING STARTED Start calculation server work), haf_client (small programming incl. class shows haf_grasping) visualization rviz: roslaunch haf_grasping haf_grasping_all.launch Publish path point cloud calculate grasp points object gripper approaching direction z-axis: rostopic pub /haf_grasping/input_pcd_rcs_path std_msgs/String “$(rospack find haf_grasping)/data/pcd2.pcd” (Alternatively, publish point cloud ros topic: /haf_grasping/depth_registered/single_cloud/points_in_lcs) EXPLANATION RVIZ VISUALIZATION RVIZ visualize point cloud frame (blue z-axis). Bigger rectangle: area heights grasp calculation rectangle: defines area grasps (grasp centers) searched. Long red line: closing direction finger gripper) Red/green spots: positions grasps tested current gripper roll (ignoring points calculation needed, data Green bars: grasps height bars grasp evaluation score higher Black arrow: grasp position approching direction parallel finger gripper) HAF-GRASPING CLIENT CODE EXPLAINDED calc_grasppoints_action_client.cpp subscribe point_cloud topic start callback point cloud code start goal (input point cloud) grasp calculation, send grasp action server receive result void CCalcGrasppointsClient::get_grasp_cb(const sensor_msgs::PointCloud2ConstPtr& pc_in) ROS_INFO(“\nFrom calc_grasppoints_action_client: point cloud received”); create action client true client spin thread actionlib::SimpleActionClient<haf_grasping::CalcGraspPointsServerAction> ac("calc_grasppoints_svm_action_server", true); ROS_INFO("Waiting action server start."); wait action server start ac.waitForServer(); wait infinite time ROS_INFO("Action server started, sending goal."); send goal action haf_grasping::CalcGraspPointsServerGoal goal; goal.graspinput.input_pc *pc_in; goal.graspinput.grasp_area_center this->graspsearchcenter; set size grasp search area goal.graspinput.grasp_area_length_x this->grasp_search_size_x+14; goal.graspinput.grasp_area_length_y this->grasp_search_size_y+14; set max grasp calculation time goal.graspinput.max_calculation_time this->grasp_calculation_time_max; //send goal ac.sendGoal(goal); //wait action return bool finished_before_timeout ac.waitForResult(ros::Duration(50.0)); (finished_before_timeout) actionlib::SimpleClientGoalState state ac.getState(); boost::shared_ptr<const haf_grasping::CalcGraspPointsServerResult_<std::allocator<void> result ac.getResult(); ROS_INFO("Result: (*(result)).result.data.c_str()); ROS_INFO("Action finished: %s",state.toString().c_str()); File truncated lines full file

description: hector_gazebo provides packages related to to simulation of robots using gazebo (gazebo plugins, world files etc.)

hector_gazebo_plugins provides gazebo plugins from Team Hector.
     Currently it contains a 6wd differential drive plugin, an IMU sensor plugin,
     an earth magnetic field sensor plugin, a GPS sensor plugin and a
     sonar ranger plugin.
hector_gazebo_thermal_camera provides a gazebo plugin that produces simulated thermal camera images. The plugin uses modified code from the gazebo_ros_camera plugin.
hector_gazebo_worlds provides gazebo scenarios used by Team Hector Darmstadt
hector_sensors_gazebo depends on the necessary plugins for using the sensors from the hector_models repository.

The hector_localization stack is a collection of packages, that provide the full 6DOF pose of a robot or platform.
    It uses various sensor sources, which are fused using an Extended Kalman filter.

    Acceleration and angular rates from an inertial measurement unit (IMU) serve as primary measurements.
    The usage of other sensors is application-dependent. The hector_localization stack currently supports
    GPS, magnetometer, barometric pressure sensors and other external sources that provide a geometry_msgs/PoseWithCovariance
    message via the poseupdate topic.
hector_pose_estimation provides the hector_pose_estimation node and the hector_pose_estimation nodelet.

hector_pose_estimation_core is the core package of the hector_localization stack. It contains the Extended Kalman Filter (EKF)
    that estimates the 6DOF pose of the robot. hector_pose_estimation can be used either as a library, as a nodelet or as a standalone node.

message_to_tf translates pose information from different kind of common_msgs message types to tf. Currently the node supports nav_msgs/Odometry, geometry_msgs/PoseStamped and sensor_msgs/Imu messages as input.
    The resulting transform is divided into three subtransforms with intermediate frames for the footprint and the stabilized base frame (without roll and pitch).
hector_components_description contains URDF xacro macros for robot components, so they are easily attachable to robot models.
hector_models contains (urdf) models of robots, sensors etc.
hector_sensors_description contains URDF xacro macros for sensors, so they are easily attachable to robot models and usable in gazebo.
hector_xacro_tools
hector_compressed_map_transport provides means for transporting compressed map data through the use of image_transport.
hector_geotiff provides a node that can be used to save occupancy grid map, robot trajectory and object of interest data to RoboCup Rescue compliant GeoTiff images.
hector_geotiff_plugins contains plugins that extend geotiff maps generated by hector_geotiff.
hector_imu_attitude_to_tf is a lightweight node that can be used to publish the roll/pitch attitude angles reported via a imu message to tf.
hector_imu_tools provides some tools for processing IMU messages

hector_map_server provides a service for retrieving the map, as well as for raycasting based obstacle queries (finds next obstacle in the map, given start and endpoint
    in any tf coordinate frame).

hector_map_tools contains some functions related to accessing information from OccupancyGridMap maps.
    Currently consists of a single header.

hector_mapping is a SLAM approach that can be used without odometry as well as on platforms that exhibit roll/pitch motion (of the sensor, the platform or both).
    It leverages the high update rate of modern LIDAR systems like the Hokuyo UTM-30LX and provides 2D pose estimates at scan rate of the sensors (40Hz for the UTM-30LX).
    While the system does not provide explicit loop closing ability, it is sufficiently accurate for many real world scenarios. The system has successfully been used on
    Unmanned Ground Robots, Unmanned Surface Vehicles, Handheld Mapping Devices and logged data from quadrotor UAVs.
hector_marker_drawing provides convenience functions for easier publishing of visualization markers.
hector_nav_msgs contains messages and services used in the hector_slam stack.
The hector_slam metapackage that installs hector_mapping and related packages.
hector_slam_launch contains launch files for launching hector_slam with different robot systems/setups/postprocessing scenarios.
hector_trajectory_server keeps track of tf trajectories extracted from tf data and makes this data accessible via a service and topic.
ROS driver node for HOKUYO 3D LIDARs

This package provides a C++ interface for camera calibration

Common code for working with images in ROS.

image_transport should always be used to subscribe to and publish images. It provides transparent
     support for transporting images in low-bandwidth compressed formats. Examples (provided by separate
     plugin packages) include JPEG/PNG compression and Theora streaming video.

polled_camera contains a service and C++ helper classes for implementing a polled
     camera driver node and requesting images from it. The package is currently for
     internal use as the API is still under development.

camera_calibration allows easy calibration of monocular or stereo
     cameras using a checkerboard calibration target.

Contains nodelets for processing depth images such as those
     produced by OpenNI camera. Functions include creating disparity
     images and point clouds, as well as registering (reprojecting)
     a depth image into another camera frame.
image_pipeline fills the gap between getting raw images from a camera driver and higher-level vision processing.
Single image rectification and color processing.

    depth images (raw, floating-point) using PNG compression.

Compressed_image_transport provides a plugin to image_transport for transparently sending images

A set of plugins for publishing and subscribing to sensor_msgs/Image topics
    in representations other than raw pixel data. For example, for viewing a
    stream of images off-robot, a video codec will give much lower bandwidth
    and latency. For low frame rate tranport of high-definition images, you
    might prefer sending them as JPEG or PNG-compressed form.

Theora_image_transport provides a plugin to image_transport for
    transparently sending an image stream encoded with the Theora codec.

Playstation 3 SIXAXIS or DUAL SHOCK 3 joystick driver.

    joysticks. In its current state, this driver is not compatible
    with the use of other Bluetooth HID devices. The driver listens

description:
The wiimote package allows ROS nodes to communicate with a Nintendo Wiimote
    and its related peripherals, including the Nunchuk, Motion Plus, and
    (experimentally) the Classic. The package implements a ROS node that uses
    Bluetooth to communicate with the Wiimote device, obtaining accelerometer
    and gyro data, the state of LEDs, the IR camera, rumble (vibrator),
    buttons, joystick, and battery state. The node additionally enables ROS
    nodes to control the Wiimote's LEDs and vibration for feedback to the human
    Wiimote operator. LEDs and vibration may be switched on and off, or made to
    operate according to a timed pattern.
authors: Andreas Paepcke, Melonee Wise, Mark Horn

The ROS package for VoiceText, ReadSpeaker (https://readspeaker.jp/)
readme: voice_text ROS Interface HOYA VoiceText Speech Synthesis Engine Installation Install VoiceText SDK voicetext sdk install binary, follow official guide install engine SDK don’t sdk install binary ReadSpeaker API binary, follow guide Install VoiceText Engine official guide Copy VoiceText API binaries VoiceText binary directory VoiceText API package includes binary libraries header file. copy specific directory executing commands. /path_to_api_package_directory ~/Downloads/RS_VTAPI_SDK_Linux_4.3.0.2/20201113_VTAPI4.3.0.2_LINUX bin/x64 system architecture Assuming VoiceText engine's talker hikari, type set directory. sudo /usr/vt/hikari/D16/bin forget add break symbolic link. Put license file Build package /path/to/catkin_workspace catkin build voice_text Usage Launch voice_text node roslaunch voice_text voice_text.launch TIPS launch VoiceText engine remote machine, creating launch file <launch> <machine name="remote" address="192.168.0.3" <include file="$(find voice_text)/launch/voice_text.launch"> <arg name="machine" value="remote" </include> </launch> speech synthesis enabled. rosrun sound_play say.py dummy robotsound:=robotsound_jp Robot Reference Service text_to_speech (voice_text/TextToSpeech) Set text_path path text file speech sentences. Set wave_path path generated wave file generated. response set true, wave file generated successfully. Parameters ~db_path (String, default: path "/var/vt/sayaka/M16")) Path VoiceText database directory. ~license_path (String, default: Path VoiceText license file. parameter empty, SDK search data-common/verify directory relative db_path. Dynamic Parameters ~pitch (Int, default: Pitch voice percentage. Speaks higher parameter higher ~speed (Int, default: Speed speech percentage. ~volume (Int, default: Volumet speech percentage. ~pause (Int, default: Pause duration sentences milliseconds.

readme: gdrive_ros Google Drive file uploader ROS Installation Setup build workspace mkdir gdrive_ws/src gdrive_ws/src git clone https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_3rdparty.git rosdep install --ignore-src -from-paths ~/gdrive_ws catkin init catkin build Trouble shooting python2.7, install versions. pip install oauth2client==4.1.3 rsa==4.5 pydrive==1.3.1 authentication Google Drive API follow step Create settings yaml follow settings yaml client_config_file: /your/client/secrets/json/path save_credentials: True save_credentials_backend: file save_credentials_file: /your/credentials/json/path get_refresh_token: True oauth_scope: https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.file set save_credentials: True, login Google account time. Run server time login Google account Run google drive server login Google account time. export GOOGLE_DRIVE_SETTINGS_YAML=/your/settings/yaml/path roslaunch gdrive_ros gdrive_server.launch Usage Run server export GOOGLE_DRIVE_SETTINGS_YAML=/your/settings/yaml/path roslaunch gdrive_ros gdrive_server.launch Call upload service single upload rosservice call /gdrive_ros/upload multiple upload rosservice call /gdrive_ros/upload_multi Parameters ~settings_yaml (string, default: PyDrive settings yaml path ~share_type (string, default: candidates: user, group, domain Uploaded file share type information, read ~share_value (string, default: Uploaded file share ~share_role (string, default: reader) Uploaded file share role ~share_with_link (bool, default: true) Uploaded file share link File truncated lines full file

description: ROS wrapper for Python SpeechRecognition library
readme: ros_speech_recognition ROS package speech-to-text services. package Python package SpeechRecognition backend. Tutorials Normal tutorial Install package SpeechReconition sudo apt install ros-${ROS_DISTRO}-ros-speech-recognition Launch speech recognition node roslaunch ros_speech_recognition speech_recognition.launch Echo /speech_to_text rostopic echo /speech_to_text recognition result Parrotry tutorial Parrotry Japanese english roslaunch ros_speech_recognition parrotry.launch japanese roslaunch ros_speech_recognition parrotry.launch language:=ja-JP speech_recognition_node.py Interface Publishing Topics ~voice_topic (speech_recognition_msgs/SpeechRecognitionCandidates) Speech recognition candidates topic Topic set parameter ~voice_topic, default speech_to_text. sound_play (sound_play/SoundRequestAction) Action client play sound events. action server ~enable_sound_effect False, sound played. Subscribing Topics ~audio_topic (audio_common_msgs/AudioData) Audio stream data recognized. Topis set parameter ~audio_topic default audio. Advertising Services speech_recognition (speech_recognition_msgs/SpeechRecognition) Service speech recognition speech_recognition/start (std_srvs/Empty) Start service speech recognition service parameter ~contiunous True. speech_recognition/start (std_srvs/Empty) Stop service speech recognition service parameter ~contiunous True. Parameters ~voice_topic (String, default: speech_to_text) Publishing voice topic ~audio_topic (String, default: audio) Subscribing audio topic ~enable_sound_effect (Bool, default: True) Flag enable disable sound play sound recognition. ~language (String, default: en-US) Language recognized ~engine (Enum[String], default: Google) Speech-to-text engine full options dynamic_reconfigure) File truncated lines full file

description: rospatlite
readme: rospatlite ROS driver patlite. Setup connect patlite computer set address computer (patlite open http://192.168.10.1/index.htm web broswser setup network stuff. manual details. roslaunch rospatlite patlite.launch IP:=IP_ADDRES enjoy patlite Usage Run patlite_node.py ros params. host hostname address patlite port port TCP connection patlite timeout timeout socket topics private namespace. rostopic list /patlite/set/blue /patlite/set/buzzer /patlite/set/green /patlite/set/red /patlite/set/white /patlite/set/yellow rostopic pub /patlite/set/buzzer std_msgs/Int8 buzzer rostopic pub /patlite/set/buzzer std_msgs/Int8 buzzer rostopic pub /patlite/set/red std_msgs/Int8 red light rostopic pub /patlite/set/yellow std_msgs/Int8 yellow light blink

description: rosping is the tool to send ICMP ECHO_REQUEST to network hosts where roscore is running, and send back to you as rostopic message.
The rostwitter package
readme: rostwitter package ROS wrapper Twitter. tweet ROS. access key API. access Twitter API. refer URL. https://developer.twitter.com/en/docs/twitter-api/getting-started/getting-access-to-the-twitter-api save yaml file format. CKEY: Consumer API Key> CSECRET: Consumer SECRET API Key> AKEY: API Key> ASECRET: API Secret Key> Launch tweet node roslaunch rostwitter tweet.launch account_info:=<PATH YAML FILE> Tweet text tweet simply publish /tweet topic. rostopic pub /tweet std_msgs/String Tweet rostwitter (https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_3rdparty)" string tweeted exceeds full-width characters half-width characters, tweeted “thread” display. rostopic pub /tweet std_msgs/String Zen Python, Tim Peters Beautiful ugly. Explicit implicit. Simple complex. Complex complicated. Flat nested. Sparse dense. Readability counts. Special cases special break rules. practicality beats purity. Errors pass silently. explicitly silenced. face ambiguity, refuse temptation guess. preferably --obvious obvious Dutch. implementation hard explain, bad idea. implementation easy explain, good idea. Namespaces honking great idea Tweet text image tweet images. base64 image path inserted text, jump reply section. Image path wget https://github.com/k-okada.png /tmp/k-okada.png rostopic pub /tweet std_msgs/String "/tmp/k-okada.png" Base64 tweet image encoding base64. python. concatenate multiple base64 images spaces. ```python import rospy import import std_msgs.msg import numpy import matplotlib.cm rostwitter.cv_util import extract_media_from_text rostwitter.cv_util import encode_image_cv2 rospy.init_node(‘rostwitter_sample’) pub rospy.Publisher(‘/tweet’, std_msgs.msg.String, queue_size=1) File truncated lines full file

description: ROS API for Sesame smart lock
readme: sesame_ros ROS API Sesame smart lock CANDY HOUSE, API key create authorization token logging CANDY HOUSE Dashboard: https://my.candyhouse.co/ Note owner Sesame order Web API. list Sesames CANDY HOUSE Dashboard. Python Package Requirements package supports catkin_virtualenv. pip dependencies written requirements.txt automatically installed virtualenv build. Scripts package list_sesames.py script listing Sesames. Arguments auth_token (required): authorization token. Path file supported. sesame_server.py node controlling Sesame. Parameters ~auth_token (string, required): authorization token. Path file supported. ~device_id (string, optional): UUID Sesame. ~nickname (string, optional): Nickname Sesame. ~device_id ~nickname searching Sesame Sesame list. matches Sesame ~command_timeout (int float, optional): Timeout executing commands. Default [sec]. Advertising services Usage section) ~get_status (sesame_ros/Status) ~lock (sesame_ros/Command) ~unlock (sesame_ros/Command) ~force_sync (sesame_ros/Command) Defined service list sesame_ros/Status string nickname string serial string device_id int32 battery bool locked bool responsive sesame_ros/Command string status bool successful string error Usage list Sesames: run rosrun sesame_ros list_sesames.py YOUR_AUTH_TOKEN controll Sesame: Start service server. description: use switchbot with ros
readme: switchbot_ros package switchbot ROS. System Component JSK members edit original file Setup switchbot Install switchbot app phone(iPhone, Android) create switchbot account. Prepare switchbot devices setup phone. set device enable cloud service. token switchbot App, profile settings, press version times token. JSK members, slide account token details installed switchbots lab. launch switchbot ros Execute roslaunch switchbot_ros switchbot.launch token:=YOUR_TOKEN publish ActionGoal. ROS API switchbot_ros node APIs ~/devices topic Type: switchbot_ros/DeviceArray topic devices list switchbot_ros server. ~/switch action Type: switchbot_ros/SwitchBotcommandAction action provide command interface switchbot devices. command line rostopic pub /switchbot_ros/switch/goal switchbot_ros/SwitchBotCommandActionGoal "header: seq: stamp: secs: nsecs: frame_id: goal_id: stamp: secs: nsecs: goal: device_name: '/eng2/7f/73b2/test/button' command: 'press' parameter: command_type: rostopic pub /switchbot_ros/switch/goal switchbot_ros/SwitchBotCommandActionGoal "header: seq: stamp: secs: nsecs: frame_id: goal_id: stamp: secs: nsecs: goal: device_name: '/myroom/light' command: 'turnOn' parameter: command_type: command details. Client libraries rospy/roseus client libraries switchbot_ros server. roseus (setq msg (get-devices)) device list (dolist (device (send msg :devices)) (format type: (send device (send device :type))) print device list (control-device "/eng2/7f/73b2/light/upper/switch" "turnOn") control device (control-device "/eng2/7f/73b2/light/lower/switch" "turnOn") rospy ```python import rospy switchbot_ros.switchbot_ros_client import SwitchBotROSClient rospy.init_node(‘hoge’) client SwitchBotROSClient() devices client.get_devices() print(devices) File truncated lines full file

description: This package provides a wrapper node for webrtcvad. It subscribes an audio topic and publish a flag if curretly speeched or not with VAD.
readme: webrtcvad_ros package wrapper node webrtcvad. subscribes audio topic publish flag curretly speeched VAD. Prerequities pip install webrtcvad roslaunch webrtcvad_ros sample.launch

description: The zdepth_image_transport package
readme: zdepth_image_transport ROS zdepth image transport plugin zdepth_image_transport faster lighter depth compression. Zdepth depth compression method zstd library. Supported format supported formats roslaunch openni2 openni2.launch rosrun image_transport republish zdepth in:=/camera/depth/image_raw raw out:=/camera/depth_decompressed/image_raw raw Visual Comparison ZDepth package) zdepth_image_transport, rgb Zdepth. description: A simple viewer for ROS image topics with draw-on features
readme: image_view2 image_view2 extended image_view. Features Draw markers image view Interact user direction Interaction Modes image_view2 support mode interact user. set interaction mode ~interaction_mode parameter change mode service interfaces. Rectangle mode (~interaction_mode:=rectangle) rectangle mode, user select region-of-interest dragging mouse pointer. Selected regions published image/screenrectangle (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped) topic. user clicks view dragging pointer, clicked position published image/screenpoint (geometry_msgs/PointStamped) topic. Line mode (~interaction_mode:=line) line mode, user select line dragging mouse pointer. Selected line published image/line (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped) topic. Polygon mode (~interaction_mode:=poly) polygon mode, user select series closed lines. User add line left-click close lines right-click. Selected polygon published image/poly (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped) topic. Grabcut mode (~interaction_mode:=grabcut) grabcut mode, user select curves. typing Esc key, clear selected curves. selected red curve published image/foreground (sensor_msgs/Image) green publiehd image/background (sensor_msgs/Image). mode originally implemented select forground background pixels grabcut. Grabcut Rectangle mode (~interaction_mode:=grabcut_rect) grabcut rectangle mode, user select rectangles. typing Esc key, clear selected rectangles. selected red rectangle published image/foreground_rect (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped) green publiehd image/background_rect (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped). mode originally implemented select forground background regions grabcut. ROS API Publising Topics image/marked (sensor_msgs/Image) Image marks drawed. image/screenrectangle_image (sensor_msgs/Image) Cropped image user selection Rectangle mode. Subscribing Topics image (sensor_msgs/Image) Input image camera_info (sensor_msgs/CameraInfo) CameraInfo image. camera info required compute projection matrix drawing markers. image_marker (image_view2/ImageMarker2) Marker topic. image/event (image_view2/MouseEvent) Event interface. image_view2 handle mouse events gui interaction. topic emulate event handling ros topics File truncated lines full file

readme: jsk_rosbag_tools Tools creating video rosbag compressing rosbag images. bag_to_video.py Create video rosbag. Usage usage: bag_to_video.py [--fps FPS] [--samplerate SAMPLERATE] [--channels CHANNELS] [--audio-topic AUDIO_TOPIC] [--image-topic IMAGE_TOPIC [IMAGE_TOPIC input_bagfile rosbag video positional arguments: input_bagfile optional arguments: show message exit output directory path filename. --image-topic interpreted directory file --fps FPS --samplerate SAMPLERATE, SAMPLERATE sampling rate --channels CHANNELS number input channels --audio-topic AUDIO_TOPIC --image-topic IMAGE_TOPIC [IMAGE_TOPIC Topic extract. rosrun jsk_rosbag_tools bag_to_video.py $(rospack find jsk_rosbag_tools)/samples/data/20220530173950_go_to_kitchen_rosbag.bag --samplerate --channels --audio-topic /audio --image-topic /head_camera/rgb/throttled/image_rect_color/compressed /tmp/20220530173950_go_to_kitchen_rosbag.mp4 bag_to_audio.py Create audio file rosbag. The cmd_vel_smoother package
readme: cmd_vel_smoother Interpolate velocity command published periodically acceleration deceleration limit. Basically node interpolates velocity violate acceleration limit decelating received velocity. control loop base_controller cmd_vel seconds, controller stops moving motors safety. move_base node publishes cmd_vel continues publishing, controllers send signal motors stop “rattle” moves suddenly stops repeatedly) damages motors hardwares. Parameters ~x_acc_lim (Double, default: limit x-axis translational acceleration. node publishes velocity violate ~y_acc_lim (Double, default: limit y-axis translational acceleration. node publishes velocity violate ~yaw_acc_lim (Double, default: limit z-axis rotational acceleration. node publishes velocity violate ~desired_rate (Double, default: node interpolates velocity commands rate ~interpolate_max_frame (Int, default: Max message count published received velocity command. Discussion package originated discussion https://github.com/ros-planning/navigation/issues/405 looked detail implementation similar nodes turtlebot robots exist: https://github.com/yujinrobot/yujin_ocs/tree/devel/yocs_velocity_smoother Author Yuki Furuta «furushchev@jsk.imi.i.u-tokyo.ac.jp»

description: The contact_states_observer package
readme: contact_states_observer Euslisp server check grasping contact states. Grasp state checking grasping state checking, hand joint angle topics. users configure setting :set-grasp-state-param joint angles success-candidates failure-candidates checked joint names. observer program checks difference candidates current hand joint angles find nearest candidate. Finally, observer program publishes results users obtain :get-contact-states. Setting parameter (list (list :rarm Success failure candidates (list (list :success (list :fail Checked joint names (list rarm setting (list :larm larm setting list Contact force checking contact force state checking, force sensor thresholding. Observer program checks current force exceeds threshold. Observer program publishes results users obtain :get-contact-states. real robot Start real robot (rtcd ros bridge servoOn) Run program roseus (progn (ros::roseus "test_grasp_check") ROBOT Initialization. HRP2JSKNTS sample (load "package://hrpsys_ros_bridge_tutorials/euslisp/hrp2jsknts-interface.l") (boundp '*robot*) (hrp2jsknts-init) (setq *robot* *hrp2jsknts*)) Loading Contact State Client (load "package://contact_states_observer/euslisp/contact-states-client.l") (setq csc (instance contact-state-client :init)) Set grasp parameter (send csc :set-grasp-state-param (list check larm. returns (list :larm) Set rarm parameter. :success hand's reset-pose, flag :fail hand's hook-pose, flag Check joint angles joints (list :rarm (list (list :success (list :fail (list Test1. Set reset-pose. return '("On" (send *robot* :hand :rarm :reset-pose) (send :hand-angle-vector (send *robot* :hand-angle-vector) (send :hand-wait-interpolation) (unix:usleep Wait hand motion stopping (format '(:rarm :larm) (send csc :get-contact-states '(:rarm :larm))) Test2. Set hook-pose. return '("Off" (send *robot* :hand :rarm :hook-pose) (send :hand-angle-vector (send *robot* :hand-angle-vector) (send :hand-wait-interpolation) (unix:usleep Wait hand motion stopping (format '(:rarm :larm) (send csc :get-contact-states '(:rarm :larm))) Publishing Topics ore_contact_states (contact_states_observer/OREContactStates) Array object, robot, environment contact state. contact state consists rleg, rarm,.. state On/Off grasping, foot, hand contact) mode grasp, contact-force, contact_grasp_states (hrpsys_ros_bridge/ContactStatesStamped) Contact states contact point rleg, lleg, rarm, larm) grasp_states (contact_states_observer/GraspState) Grasp state

Implement plugin Finally, implement jsk_teleop_joy plugin. description: Convert audio (audio_common_msgs/AudioData) to spectrogram (sensor_msgs/Image)
readme: audio_to_spectrogram package converts audio data time-series data) spectrum spectrogram data. Usage command, publish audio, spectrum spectrogram topics. set correct args microphone configuration, mic_sampling_rate bitdepth. roslaunch audio_to_spectrogram audio_to_spectrogram.launch data conversion pipeline audio_to_spectrum.py spectrum normalized_half_spectrum log_spectrum preprocess node(s) preprocessed spectrum spectrum_to_spectrogram.py spectrogram rosbag audio. roslaunch audio_to_spectrogram sample_audio_to_spectrogram.launch Audio Amplitude Spectrum Spectrogram convert data audio spectrum spectrogram data package. rosbag force torque sensor sensing drill vibration. roslaunch audio_to_spectrogram sample_wrench_to_spectrogram.launch Z-axis Force Amplitude Normalized Spectrum Spectrogram Source Spectrum Spectrogram Scripts audio_to_spectrum.py script convert audio spectrum. Publishing topics ~spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Spectrum data calculated audio FFT. usual “amplitude spectrum”. https://ryo-iijima.com/fftresult/ details. ~normalized_half_spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Spectrum data non-negative frequencies (0Hz-Nyquist frequency)) “normalized” (consistent amplitude original signal). details. https://ryo-iijima.com/fftresult/ https://stackoverflow.com/questions/63211851/why-divide-the-output-of-numpy-fft-by-n https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_recognition/issues/2761#issue-1550715400 ~log_spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Log-scaled spectrum data. calculated applying log absolute FFT result. log applied “power spectrum”, don’t simplicity. details. https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_recognition/issues/2761#issuecomment-1445810380 http://makotomurakami.com/blog/2020/05/23/5266/ Subscribing topics ~audio (audio_common_msgs/AudioData) Audio stream data microphone. audio format wave. Parameters ~mic_sampling_rate (Int, default: Sampling rate microphone. sampling rate audio topic. ~fft_sampling_period (Double, default: Period sample audio data FFT. ~n_channel (Int, default: Number channel microphone. ~target_channel (Int, default: Target channel. ~bitdepth (Int, default: Number bits audio data. ~fft_exec_rate (Double, default: Rate execute FFT publish results. data_to_spectrum.py Generalized version audio_to_spectrum.py. Ubuntu Ubuntu Create workspace mkdir ~/sound_classification_ws/src ~/sound_classification_ws/src git clone https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_recognition.git rosdep install --from-paths --ignore-src catkin build sound_classification source ~/sound_classification_ws/devel/setup.bash Install packages. cuda cupy needed chainer. installation guide JSK GPU highly recommended. Usage Check microphone parameters. device, n_channel, bitdepth mic_sampling_rate bash commands params device. card device device:="hw:0,0" arecord List CAPTURE Hardware Devices card PCH [HDA Intel PCH], device ALC293 Analog [ALC293 Analog] Subdevices: Subdevice subdevice n_channel, bitdepth sample_rate, Note sources means input microphone) sinks means output speaker) pactl list short sources alsa_input.pci-0000_00_1f.3.analog-stereo module-alsa-card.c s16le SUSPENDED Pass params launch file arguments launching `device:=hw:0,0 n_channel:=2 bitdepth:=16 mic_sampling_rate:=44100`). `/audio` topic computer publish `/audio`, set `use_microphone:=false` launch file launching. Horiozntal axis time Vertical axis frequency |Spectrogram noise subtraction|Spectrogram noise subtraction| |![](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19769486/86824253-d9e6df80-c0c8-11ea-8946-ca1367c1b1b0.png)|![](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19769486/86824246-d81d1c00-c0c8-11ea-8c13-dc9660e89ea0.png)| Collect spectrogram classify. volume exceeds threshold, save spectrogram train_data/original_spectrogram/TARGET_CLASS. rosbag stream sound sources. Rosbag version (Recommended) recommend rosbag collect spectrograms. rosbag easy save_sound.launch parameters. target_class:=TARGET_CLASS, set class target sound. use_rosbag:=true filename:=PATH_TO_ROSBAG, save spectrograms rosbag. default, rosbag paused Press ‘Space’ key terminal start playing rosbag. rosbag ends, press ‘Ctrl-c’ terminate. newly saved spectrograms appended existing spectrograms. change threshold sound saving threshold:=xxx. smaller easily sound saved. Save audio rosbag roslaunch sound_classification record_audio_rosbag.launch filename:=PATH_TO_ROSBAG play rosbag collecting data export ROS_MASTER_URI=http://localhost:11311 roslaunch sound_classification save_sound.launch use_rosbag:=true filename:=PATH_TO_ROSBAG target_class:=TARGET_CLASS threshold:=0.5 setting `threshold:=0` `save_when_sound:=false`, collect spectrogram sound". ```bash File truncated lines full file

Provides nodes to assemble point clouds from either LaserScan or PointCloud messages
ROS Libraries for filtering specific kinds of laser scans
Filters a laser scan to remove points that are in the current static map

Assorted filters designed to operate on 2D planar laser scanners,
    which use the sensor_msgs/LaserScan type.

This package contains a class for converting from a 2D laser scan as defined by
    sensor_msgs/LaserScan into a point cloud as defined by sensor_msgs/PointCloud
    or sensor_msgs/PointCloud2. In particular, it contains functionality to account
    for the skew resulting from moving robots or tilting laser scanners.

Meta-package of libraries for processing laser data, including converting laser data
      into 3D representations.

The marker_msgs package contains messages usable to setup a marker/fiducial system. 
    The package distinguishes between two types of messages. 
    First messages to describe the properties of a marker/fiducial detection system and the detected markers. 
    Secondly messages used to represent a map of markers/features with covariances as it would be produced by a SLAM system or published by a map server for self-localization.

description: This package provides implementations of the Trajectory Rollout and Dynamic Window approaches to local robot navigation on a plane. Given a plan to follow and a costmap, the controller produces velocity commands to send to a mobile base. This package supports both holonomic and non-holonomic robots, any robot footprint that can be represented as a convex polygon or circle, and exposes its configuration as ROS parameters that can be set in a launch file. This package's ROS wrapper adheres to the BaseLocalPlanner interface specified in the

description: This planner attempts to find a legal place to put a carrot for the robot to follow. It does this by moving back along the vector between the robot and the goal point.

description: This package provides a recovery behavior for the navigation stack that attempts to clear space by reverting the costmaps used by the navigation stack to the static map outside of a given area.

This package provides an implementation of a 2D costmap that takes in sensor
        data from the world, builds a 2D or 3D occupancy grid of the data (depending
        on whether a voxel based implementation is used), and inflates costs in a
        2D costmap based on the occupancy grid and a user specified inflation radius.
        This package also provides support for map_server based initialization of a
        costmap, rolling window based costmaps, and parameter based subscription to
        and configuration of sensor topics.
authors: Eitan Marder-Eppstein, David V. Lu!!, Dave Hershberger, contradict <AT> gmail.com

This package provides common interfaces for navigation specific robot actions. Currently, this package provides the BaseGlobalPlanner, BaseLocalPlanner, and RecoveryBehavior interfaces, which can be used to build actions that can easily swap their planner, local controller, or recovery behavior for new versions adhering to the same interface.

navfn provides a fast interpolated navigation function that can be used to create plans for
        a mobile base. The planner assumes a circular robot and operates on a costmap to find a
        minimum cost plan from a start point to an end point in a grid. The navigation function is
        computed with Dijkstra's algorithm, but support for an A* heuristic may also be added in the
        near future. navfn also provides a ROS wrapper for the navfn planner that adheres to the
        nav_core::BaseGlobalPlanner interface specified in
authors: Kurt Konolige, Eitan Marder-Eppstein, contradict <AT> gmail.com

description:
A 2D navigation stack that takes in information from odometry, sensor
        streams, and a goal pose and outputs safe velocity commands that are sent
        to a mobile base.
readme: ROS Navigation Stack navigation stack takes information odometry, sensor streams, goal pose outputs safe velocity commands mobile base. Related stacks: http://github.com/ros-planning/navigation_tutorials http://github.com/ros-planning/navigation_experimental

description: The Robot Pose EKF package is used to estimate the 3D pose of a robot, based on (partial) pose measurements coming from different sources. It uses an extended Kalman filter with a 6D model (3D position and 3D orientation) to combine measurements from wheel odometry, IMU sensor and visual odometry. The basic idea is to offer loosely coupled integration with different sensors, where sensor signals are received as ROS messages.
This package provides a recovery behavior for the navigation stack that attempts to clear space by performing a 360 degree rotation of the robot.

voxel_grid provides an implementation of an efficient 3D voxel grid. The occupancy grid can support 3 different representations for the state of a cell: marked, free, or unknown. Due to the underlying implementation relying on bitwise and and or integer operations, the voxel grid only supports 16 different levels per voxel column. However, this limitation yields raytracing and cell marking performance in the grid comparable to standard 2D structures making it quite fast compared to most 3D structures.
authors: Eitan Marder-Eppstein, Eric Berger, contradict <AT> gmail.com

description: Meta-Package containing modules for 2D-Navigation

This package holds a collection of plugins for the RobotNavigator, that provide
    different cooperative exploration strategies for a team of mobile robots.

Graph-based Simultaneous Localization and Mapping module.
    Includes OpenKarto GraphSLAM library by "SRI International".

Wrapper around Particle Filter implementation.
    The SelfLocalizer can be used as library or as a ros-node.
Messages used for 2D-Navigation.

This package provides a node for higher level navigation of a mobile
    robot in a planar environment. It needs a map and the robot's position
    within this map to create a plan for navigation. When used together with
    a SLAM module it can also be used to perform autonomous exploration of
    the robot's workspace.

The operator is a lightweight, purely reactive obstacle-avoidance
    module for mobile robots moving in a planar environment. The operator node
    works by evaluating a set of predefined motion primitives based on a local
    costmap and a desired direction. The best evaluated motion command will be
    send to the mobile base.

This package is used to manually control a robot that uses the operator and
    navigator node from navigation_2d. Currently there is one node to control one
    robot with a joystick and one to control multiple robots in simulation.
    It can send commands directly to the operator or start and stop navigator actions.

description:
Contains a set of tools that can be used from a hard
    realtime thread, without breaking the realtime behavior.  The
    tools currently only provides the realtime publisher, which makes
    it possible to publish messages to a ROS topic from a realtime
    thread. We plan to add a basic implementation of a realtime
    buffer, to make it possible to get data from a (non-realtime)
    topic callback into the realtime loop. Once the lockfree buffer is
    created, the realtime publisher will start using it, which will
    result in major API changes for the realtime publisher (removal of
    all lock methods).

description: A ROS driver for the SICK TiM series of laser scanners.

This package contains a ROS wrapper for OpenSlam's Gmapping. 
  The gmapping package provides laser-based SLAM (Simultaneous Localization and Mapping), 
  as a ROS node called slam_gmapping. Using slam_gmapping, you can create a 2-D occupancy
  grid map (like a building floorplan) from laser and pose data collected by a mobile robot.
slam_gmapping contains a wrapper around gmapping which provides SLAM capabilities.

description:
The teb_local_planner package implements a plugin
	to the base_local_planner of the 2D navigation stack.
	The underlying method called Timed Elastic Band locally optimizes
	the robot's trajectory with respect to trajectory execution time,
	separation from obstacles and compliance with kinodynamic constraints at runtime.

description:
TRAC-IK is a faster, significantly more reliable drop-in replacement for
    KDL's pseudoinverse Jacobian solver.

    The TRAC-IK library has a very similar API to KDL's IK solver calls,
    except that the user passes a maximum time instead of a maximum number of
    search iterations.  Additionally, TRAC-IK allows for error tolerances to
    be set independently for each Cartesian dimension (x,y,z,roll,pitch.yaw).
readme: TRAC-IK kinematics solver built trac_ik_lib library tested ROS Indigo Catkin). headers shared objects package linked user programs. package part ROS Noetic binaries: sudo apt-get install ros-noetic-trac-ik package part ROS Indigo/Jade binaries: sudo apt-get install ros-jade-trac-ik requires Ubuntu packages NLOpt Libraries installed ros-indigo-nlopt packages proper headers). running sudo apt-get install libnlopt-dev trusty standard Ubuntu distros. Alternatively, run ```rosdep update rosdep install trac_ik_lib KDL KDL::ChainFkSolverPos_recursive fk_solver(chain); KDL::ChainIkSolverVel_pinv vik_solver(chain); KDL::ChainJntToJacSolver jac_solver(chain); KDL::ChainIkSolverPos_NR_JL ik_solver(KDL::Chain chain, KDL::JntArray lower_joint_limits, KDL::JntArray upper_joint_limits, fk_solver, vik_solver, int num_iterations, double error); int ik_solver.CartToJnt(KDL::JntArray joint_seed, KDL::Frame desired_end_effector_pose, KDL::JntArray& return_joints); NOTE: CartToJnt succeeded NOTE: timeout seconds iterations set called loop timer. NOTE: error acceptable purposes TRAC-IK: #include <trac_ik/trac_ik.hpp> TRAC_IK::TRAC_IK ik_solver(KDL::Chain chain, KDL::JntArray lower_joint_limits, KDL::JntArray upper_joint_limits, double timeout_in_secs=0.005, double error=1e-5, TRAC_IK::SolveType type=TRAC_IK::Speed); TRAC_IK::TRAC_IK ik_solver(string base_link, string tip_link, string URDF_param="/robot_description", double timeout_in_secs=0.005, double error=1e-5, TRAC_IK::SolveType type=TRAC_IK::Speed); NOTE: arguments constructors optional. type Speed: returns quickly solution Distance: runs full timeout_in_secs, returns solution minimizes SSE seed Manip1: runs full timeout, returns solution maximizes sqrt(det(J*J^T)) product singular values Jacobian) Manip2: runs full timeout, returns solution minimizes ratio min max singular values Jacobian. int ik_solver.CartToJnt(KDL::JntArray joint_seed, KDL::Frame desired_end_effector_pose, KDL::JntArray& return_joints, KDL::Twist tolerances); NOTE: CartToJnt succeeded NOTE: tolerances effector pose optional, default ABS() values set tolerances -tol..0..+tol Cartesian dimensions effector pose.

description:
This contains CvBridge, which converts between ROS
    Image messages and OpenCV images.

`image_geometry` contains C++ and Python libraries for interpreting images
    geometrically. It interfaces the calibration parameters in sensor_msgs/CameraInfo
    messages with OpenCV functions such as image rectification, much as cv_bridge
    interfaces ROS sensor_msgs/Image with OpenCV data types.
Tests the enumerants of the ROS Image message, and functionally tests the Python and C++ implementations of CvBridge.
Packages for interfacing ROS with OpenCV, a library of programming functions for real time computer vision.
Virtual package providing ViSP related packages.

Online automated pattern-based object tracker relying on visual servoing.

    visp_auto_tracker wraps model-based trackers provided by ViSP visual
    servoing library into a ROS package. The tracked object should have a
    QRcode of Flash code pattern. Based on the pattern, the object is
    automaticaly detected. The detection allows then to initialise the
    model-based trackers. When lost of tracking achieves a new detection
    is performed that will be used to re-initialize the tracker.

    This computer vision algorithm computes the pose (i.e. position and
    orientation) of an object in an image. It is fast enough to allow
    object online tracking using a camera.
readme: visp_auto_tracker visp_auto_tracker wraps model-based trackers ViSP visual servoing library ROS package. tracked object QRcode Flash code pattern. Based pattern, object automaticaly detected. detection initialise model-based trackers. lost tracking achieves detection performed re-initialize tracker. computer vision algorithm computes pose position orientation) object image. fast object online tracking camera. package composed node called ‘visp_auto_tracker’. node detect QRcode Flash code object. detection performed, node tracks object. lost tracking occurs node detect object restart tracking. viewer comming visp_tracker package monitor tracking result. Project webpage ros.org: tutorial API reference Project webpage: source code download, bug report Setup package submodules. compiled ROS package catkin_make. Prerequisities visp_auto_tracker depends visp_bridge visp_tracker packages https://github.com/lagadic/vision_visp (indigo-devel branches). visp_auto_tracker depends libdmtx-dev libzbar-dev system dependencies. install run: sudo apt-get install libdmtx-dev libzbar-dev build visp_tracker Supposed catkin work space run: ~/catkin_ws/src git clone indigo-devel https://github.com/lagadic/vision_visp.git catkin_make --pkg visp_auto_tracker Documentation documentation project ROS homepage. information, refer ROS tutorial.

description:
This package contains a recent version of the Bayesian Filtering
  Library (BFL), distributed by the Orocos Project.  For stability
  reasons, this package is currently locked to revision 31655 (April
  19, 2010), but this revision will be updated on a regular basis to
  the latest available BFL trunk.  This ROS package does not modify
  BFL in any way, it simply provides a convenient way to download and
  compile the library, because BFL is not available from an OS package
  manager.  This ROS package compiles BFL with the Boost library for
  matrix operations and random number generation.
authors: Klaas Gadeyne, Wim Meeussen, Tinne Delaet and many others. See web page for a full contributor list. ROS package maintained by Wim Meeussen.

This allows to easily integrate the visualization tool with existing control architectures.

(Note, nice message specifies type input Odometry bit hack.) Tranforms message Odometry message express information frames utm odom Broadcasts tranforms utm->odom odom->base_link use-case motivated project integrating sensors provide GPS-aided INS solution microstrain, advanced navigation, Xsens, situation analogous ekf/ukf node robot_localization package fuse IMU GPS information, case processing sensor. purpose package integration type sensor ROS navigation stack. Parameters ~datum: origin local “odom” frame element array [Latitude, Logitude, Altitude]. Lat/Lon decimal degrees; altitude meters. Default ~frequency: frequency broadcasting tranforms. Odometry messages published rate incoming Odometry messages. Default ~broadcast_utm2odom_transform: broadcast utm->odom tranform. Default True. ~broadcast_odom2base_transform: broadcast odom->base_link tranform. Default True. ~zero_altitude ~base_link_frame_id: Default “base_link” ~odom_frame_id: Default “odom” ~utm_frame_id: Default “utm” Subscribed Topics /odometry/nav: nav_msgs/Odometry message geographic position velocity data. message organized header.frame_id child_frame_id values pose.pose.position Latitude [dec. degrees] Longitude [dec. degrees] Altitude pose.pose.orientation base_link relative fixed ENU coordinate frame ~orientation_ned parameter set true, node convert orientation NED ENU. assuming orientation true magnetic). Typically magnetic declination set internal sensor providing information. pose.covariance expressed meters position radians orientation (REP-103) twist.twist.linear/angular velocity base_link frame twist.covariance expressed rad/s. Published Topics message published rate incoming /odometry/nav messages. Consistent navigation stack robot_localization, Odometry messages position pose information relative fixed frame (utm odom, reported header.frame_id). velocity (twist) information relative mobile frame (base_link, reported .child_frame). /odometry/odom: nav_msgs/Odometry message local odom frame (relative datum) /odometry/utm: nav_msgs/Odometry message UTM frame published static tranform: http://wiki.ros.org/tf2_ros http://wiki.ros.org/tf2/Tutorials/Writing%20a%20tf2%20static%20broadcaster%20(C%2B%2B) Published Transforms utm->odom odom->base_link Frames utm: global UTM coordinate frame. origin frame UTM zone determined datum parameter odom: local, fixed odom frame orgin datum parameter. assumed orientation UTM odom frame. general simplifies implementation, usage interpretation. base_link: mobile frame typically coincides sensor frame.

hector_object_tracker is the core package of hector_worldmodel. It listens to percept message from
     detectors (e.g. heat signatures of persons or recognitions of other objects of interest in the
     scene) and fuses all information to a single worldmodel state. Objects will be tracked over time
     and their states can be influenced by a couple of services.

     The percept to object association problem is solved either automatically based on the Mahalanobis
     distance, or a unique object_id can be given in the percept message.

     If a hector_nav_msgs/GetDistanceToObstacle service is available, the object_tracker can optionally
     deduce the depth of objects in the scene by projection to the nearest obstacle (wall).

The hector_worldmodel stack helps to collect and fuse information about objects in the world.
    It is used by Team Hector Darmstadt to localize victims and QR codes in the Robocup Rescue
    scenario. See

hector_worldmodel_geotiff_plugins contains a plugin to visualize worldmodel objects (e.g. victims in a
     Search and Rescue scenario) in a geotiff map generated by

hector_worldmodel_msgs is a message package to comes with the hector_worldmodel stack.
     The messages can be used to send percepts from images (hector_worldmodel_msgs/ImagePercept) or other sources
     (hector_worldmodel_msgs/PosePercept) to the hector_object_tracker node. The tracker publishes model updates as
     hector_worldmodel_msgs/Object messages and latches the whole model state as a hector_worldmodel_msgs/ObjectModel message.

description: Implementation of the MultiWii Serial Protocol (MSP)
readme: MultiWii Cleanflight Baseflight Communication Library library implements MultiWii Serial Protocol (MSP) communicating MultiWii Cleanflight flight controller serial device. defines low-level API sending+encoding receiving+decoding MSP messages, high-level API subscriber pattern periodically request data call callback functions message received. communication tested MultiWii Arduino Nano achieve update rate approximately cycle time Betaflight Naze32 Rev6 update rates max. Installation Test Linux (Ubuntu Debian) install asio ninja: sudo apt install --no-install-recommends ninja-build libasio-dev check source code cmake compile: cmake build -GNinja -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release cmake --build build run program path serial device: ./msp_read_test /dev/ttyUSB0 Windows Requirements CMake asio: download asio-1.20.0.zip extract header files, C:\asio-1.20.0 Visual Build Tools: https://visualstudio.microsoft.com/de/downloads/ Build Test open Developer Command Prompt Visual Studio change directory checked msp run: cmake build -GNinja -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DASIO_ROOT=C:\asio-1.20.0 cmake --build build test serial device: msp_read_test.exe Hardware Setup connect Arduino Naze32 boards built-in USB-to-Serial connector direct serial connection (RX->TX, TX->RX, GND->GND, VCC->VCC). option preferable high transfer rates, direct connection exposed transmission errors. MultiWii MSP update rate determined variable LOOP_TIME config.h #define LOOP_TIME sets loop time update rate Arduino boards integrated USB-to-serial converter auto-reset serial connection established. send messages opening connection request send response. prevent delay opening connection sending messages, disabling Data Terminal Ready (DTR) line, stty /dev/ttyUSB0 -hupcl. description: pioneer_bringup provides roslaunch scripts for starting the core functionnalities of Adept MobileRobots Pioneer and Pioneer-compatible robots (Including Pioneer 2, Pioneer 3, Pioneer LX, AmigoBot, PeopleBot, PatrolBot, PowerBot, Seekur and Seekur Jr.)
readme: Pionner_bringup ROS package providing roslaunch functionalities starting Adept MobileRobots Pioneer Pioneer-compatible robots (Including Pioneer Pioneer Pioneer AmigoBot, PeopleBot, PatrolBot, PowerBot, Seekur Seekur Compatible RosAria P2OS hardware controllers. Installation Depending install dependencies: ROSARIA P2OS Urg_node: Hokuyo lidars LMS1XX: Sick LMS100 lidar Sicktoolbox_wrapper: Sick LMS2xx lidar Sick_scan: Sick lidars list bellow) Usbcam: usb cameras webcams Freenect: Kinect Pionner_dashboard: GUI dashboard window Usage starting robot default parameters: roslaunch pioneer_bringup pioneer_bringup.launch activate features arguments: Argument values Default Description controller rosaria, p2os rosaria Hardware driver controller controller_port /dev/ttySx /dev/ttyUSBx /dev/ttyS0 Serial port communicate controller sonar true, false false Activate sonars laser hokuyo, lms_1xx, lms_2xx, lms_1xxx, lms_4xxx, lms_5xx, mrs_1xxx, mrs_6xxx, rms_3xx, tim_5xx, tim_7xx, tim_7xxS false Laser reference code ‘false’ laser usbcam true, false false launch usbcam node kinect true, false false launch kinect nodes dashboard true, false false launch GUI window rqt visualize sensors readings control motors roslaunch pioneer_bringup pioneer_bringup.launch controller:=p2os laser:=lms_5xx usbcam:=true controls Laser node: arguments lidar parameters: Argument values Default Description laser_hostname Address Laser address hostname applicable) laser_serial_port /dev/ttySx /dev/ttyUSBx /dev/ttyUSB0 Laser Port applicable) laser_baselink_distance roll pitch yaw Distance baselink lidar meters setting broadcast) roslaunch pioneer_bringup pioneer_bringup.launch laser:=lms_1xx laser_hostname:=192.168.0.4 laser_baselink_distance:='0.20 controls Camera node: arguments customize camera parameters: Argument Values Default Description camera_port /dev/videox device number) /dev/video0 USB Camera port camera_display true, false true Display video feed screen stop parameters: customize parameters config/camera.yaml file, image_width, image_height, pixel_format, camera_frame_id io_method. roslaunch pioneer_bringup pioneer_bringup.launch usbcam:=true camera_display:=false camera_port:=/dev/video2 controls Kinect node: arguments customize kinect parameters: Argument Values Default Description kinect_data_skip Number frames skip kinect data kinect_id kinect device number roslaunch pioneer_bringup pioneer_bringup.launch kinect:=true kinect_data_skip:=5 issues open /dev/ttyUSB0: Permission denied frequent issue USB-Serial adapter connect robot hardware lidar. Basically give correct autorizations: sudo chmod /dev/ttyUSB0 issues report problem issues section

description: The pioneer_teleop package provides teleoperation using keyboard, sockets or command line for the Adept MobileRobots Pioneer and Pioneer-compatible robots (Including Pioneer 2, Pioneer 3, Pioneer LX, AmigoBot, PeopleBot, PatrolBot, PowerBot, Seekur and Seekur Jr.).
readme: Pionner_teleop ROS package providing scripts teleoperation keyboard, sockets command line. package compatible robot ROS ecosystem, originally implemented Adept MobileRobots Pioneer Pioneer-compatible robots (Including Pioneer Pioneer Pioneer AmigoBot, PeopleBot, PatrolBot, PowerBot, Seekur Seekur case robot, read robot Pionner-compatible?” section Note: package tested ROS Kinetic Kame Installation Dependencies install pioneer_bringup package, https://github.com/amine_horseman/pionner_bringup.git Source Download clone repository caktin workspace: ~/catkin_ws/src git clone https://github.com/amineHorseman/pioneer_teleop.git rosdep install pioneer_teleop Build catkin packages source ~/catkin_ws catkin_make python scripts executable ~/catkin_ws/src/pioneer_teleop/nodes sudo chmod Usage ##Teleop Modes package, ways teleoperation: Keyboard teleoperation: roslaunch pioneer_teleop keyboard_teleop.launch control robot motors keyboard arrows, increase decrease speed, stop version keyboard teleoperation command: roslaunch pioneer_teleop discrete_keyboard_teleop.launch case, robot moves small period time seconds default) stops. Sockets teleoperation: Controls robot remotely throught socket commands move robot web interface internet/LAN). roslaunch pionner_teleop socket_teleop.launch expected commands “left” default, script listens port robot moves seconds. change parameters, extra arguments mentionned bellow: roslaunch pionner_teleop socket_teleop.launch _port:=12345 _speed:=0.3 _move_time:=2.0 Command line teleoperation move robot command line throught terminal ssh roslaunch pionner_teleop socket_commandline.launch _direction:=forward expected commands (_direction argument) “left” default, robot moves seconds speed. change parameters, extra arguments mentionned bellow: roslaunch pionner_teleop socket_teleop.launch _direction:=backward _speed:=0.3 _move_time:=2.0 robot Pionner-compatible? package compatible robot ROS long velocity commands published /cmd_vel topic section). modify .launch scripts remove pionner_bringup call, execute python scripts located /nodes folder. issues Velocity command topic default, scripts publish velocity commands /cmd_vel topic. case velocity commands topic Pionner-compatible robots, remap velocity topic /cmd_vel change topic python scripts /nodes folder issues report problem issues panel File truncated lines full file

Install package depends gazebo_ros_demos rrbot_description package, add catkin workspace source: git clone https://github.com/ros-simulation/gazebo_ros_demos.git install package source develop install debian: sudo apt-get install ros-indigo-ros-control-boilerplate Run Simulation Demo package setup run “RRBot” joint revolute-revolute robot demo. “template package” located ros_control_boilerplate subfolder easily rename reuse. run ros_control non-physics-based simulated hardware interface, run: roslaunch ros_control_boilerplate rrbot_simulaton.launch visualize published coordinate transforms Rviz run: roslaunch ros_control_boilerplate rrbot_visualize.launch send random, dummy trajectory execute, run: roslaunch ros_control_boilerplate rrbot_test_trajectory.launch Customize test simulation interface robot, quickly rename subfolder package robot commands: function findreplace() grep xargs sed function findreplacefilename() find -depth -exec bash 'for base=${f##*/}; "${f%/*}/${base//'$1'/'$2'}"; done' findreplacefilename rrbot myrobot findreplace rrbot myrobot findreplace RRBot MyRobot findreplace RRBOT MYROBOT add code communicate robot USB/serial/ethernet/etc file myrobot_hw_interface.cpp. Setting Initial Position, MoveIt! robot startup position simulation, funcitonality simulate robot MoveIt!, downstream package depends package) moveit_sim_controller Helper Tools Recording CSV Write commands trajectory controller csv file rosrun ros_control_boilerplate controller_to_csv SAVE_TO_FILE_PATH CONTROLLER_STATE_TOPIC TIME_TO_RECORD Commanding CSV Read csv file execute robot rosrun ros_control_boilerplate csv_to_controller READ_FROM_FILE_PATH CONTROLLER_STATE_TOPIC TIME_TO_RECORD Commanding Keyboard Joint-level teleop keyboard (TODO: remove coded topic names) rosrun ros_control_boilerplate keyboard_teleop Limitations implement estops, transmissions, fancy features ros_contorl hard realtime code, depends largely platform, kernel, File truncated lines full file

description: Quickly load variables from rosparam with good command line error checking.
readme: ROS Param Shortcuts Quickly load variables rosparam good command line error checking. package enforces philosophy default parameters defined user yaml files launch files program run. helps debug isn’t working correctly rosparameters missing. Features: Outputs loaded data consule ROS_DEBUG, won’t turn Namespaces output parent_name Great class parameter namespace Helpful error messages parameter missing, explaining expects find Removes lots repetitious code Supports datatypes rosparam default, std::size_t, ros::Duration, Eigen::Affine3d Supports loading std::vectors easily, debugging data Supports loading entire list bool parameters Developed Dave Coleman University Colorado Boulder Status: Travis Continuous Integration ROS Buildfarm Trusty Devel Source Build ROS Buildfarm AMD64 Trusty Debian Build Install Ubuntu Debian sudo apt-get install ros-indigo-rosparam-shortcuts Build Source build package, git clone repo catkin workspace install dependencies running command root catkin workspace: rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src --rosdistro indigo Code API Class Reference Usage Demo file src/example.cpp code. run: roslaunch rosparam_shortcuts example.launch yaml file file config/example.yaml: control_rate: double param1: int param2: size_t param3: ros::Duration param4: Eigen::Affine3d roll, pitch, yaw param5: std::vector<double> Include Note dependency issues issue easy fix, including package package add dependencies Eigen throught package. requires: package.xml: <build_depend>eigen</build_depend> <run_depend>eigen</run_depend> CMakeLists.txt: find_package(catkin REQUIRED COMPONENTS cmake_modules find_package(Eigen REQUIRED) catkin_package( DEPENDS Eigen include_directories( ${EIGEN_INCLUDE_DIRS} fix Testing Linting run roslint, command catkin-tools: catkin build --no-status --no-deps --make-args roslint run catkin lint, command catkin-tools: File truncated lines full file

readme: rtt_dynamic_reconfigure package manipulate properties Orocos RTT component ROS dynamic_reconfigure interface.
