Simply add dynamic_reconfigure server component loading reconfigure service: import("rtt_dynamic_reconfigure"); loadService("my_component", "reconfigure") my_component.reconfigure.advertise("/my_component") disadvantage method minimum maximum values requrired dynamic_reconfigure unknown. current values properties advertised default values. overwrite automatically choosen minimum/maximum values calling advertise() operation: import("rtt_dynamic_reconfigure"); loadService("my_component", "reconfigure") my_component.reconfigure.min.int_property my_component.reconfigure.max.int_property my_component.reconfigure.advertise("~/my_component") custom config file rtt_dynamic_reconfigure package templated rtt_dynamic_reconfigure::Server<ConfigType> class implements core functionality dynamic_reconfigure server. ConfigType class header generated automatically *.cfg file dynamic_reconfigure’s generate_dynamic_reconfigure_options() cmake macro. config file describes parameters minimum, maximum default values. order rtt_dynamic_reconfigure custom config, create .cfg file explained tutorial. add rtt_dynamic_reconfigure service plugin package: project(my_package) find_package(dynamic_reconfigure) generate_dynamic_reconfigure_options(cfg/MyPackage.cfg) orocos_plugin(my_package_reconfigure_service src/reconfigure_service.cpp) add_dependencies(rtt_dynamic_reconfigure_tests_service ${PROJECT_NAME}_gencfg) reconfigure_service.cpp source file instantiates rtt_dynamic_reconfigure::Server<ConfigType> config type MyPackageConfig, implements rtt_dynamic_reconfigure::Updater<ConfigType> class explains fill config PropertyBag vice-versa registers server service plugin: #include <rtt_dynamic_reconfigure/server.h> #include <my_package/MyPackageConfig.h> header created generate_dynamic_reconfigure_options(cfg/MyPackage.cfg) namespace my_package; namespace rtt_dynamic_reconfigure template struct Updater<MyPackageConfig> static bool propertiesFromConfig(MyPackageConfig &config, uint32_t level, RTT::PropertyBag &bag) setProperty<int>("int_param", bag, config.int_param); setProperty<double>("double_param", bag, config.double_param); setProperty<std::string>("str_param", bag, config.str_param); setProperty<bool>("bool_param", bag, config.bool_param); return true; static bool configFromProperties(MyPackageConfig &config, const RTT::PropertyBag &bag) getProperty<int>("int_param", bag, config.int_param); getProperty<double>("double_param", bag, config.double_param); getProperty<std::string>("str_param", bag, config.str_param); getProperty<bool>("bool_param", bag, config.bool_param); return true; namespace rtt_dynamic_reconfigure RTT_DYNAMIC_RECONFIGURE_SERVICE_PLUGIN(MyPackageConfig, "my_package_reconfigure") rtt_dynamic_reconfigure::setProperty<T>(...) rtt_dynamic_reconfigure::getProperty<T>(...) helper functions Updater implementation. Publishing subscribing command, topic type inferred Orocos port type. Connection policies created operations: rostopic.connection(TOPIC_NAME): Creates connection buffer length rostopic.bufferedConnection(TOPIC_NAME, BUFFER_LENGTH): Creates connection user-supplied buffer length. rostopic.unbufferedConnection(TOPIC_NAME): Creates unbuffered connection, writing thread immediately publishs message (publishing real-time safe. Note TOPIC_NAME prefixed tilde resolved process’s private namespace, similarly topic names resolved rospy. Note function RTT operation operation operation caller. RTT_OPERATION_NAME: task-scoped operation/operation caller provided/required services separated dots foo.bar.baz.op) ROS_SERVICE_NAME: service client/server ROS graph /some/ros/ns/my_service) ROS_SERVICE_TYPE: full typename service std_srvs/Empty) rosservice.disconnect(ROS_SERVICE_NAME) Disconnects RTT operation operation caller ROS service server client.. ROS_SERVICE_NAME: service client/server ROS graph /some/ros/ns/my_service) rosservice.disconnectAll() Disconnects RTT operations operation callers ROS service servers clients. global RTT service rosservice_registry operations: rosservice_registry.registerServiceFactory(FACTORY): Register ROS service factory rosservice_registry.hasServiceFactory(TYPENAME): Check ROS service type registered rosservice_registry.geServiceFactory(TYPENAME): ROS service client/server factory Code Generation package facilities generating typekits ROS service types defined .srv files generating plugins register ROS service types rosservice_registry service. description: This package provides an RTT service and service-requester for associating RTT component properties with ROS parameters
readme: RTT ROSParam package RTT service service-requester associating RTT component properties ROS parameters. Design RTT service enables setting/getting individual RTT properties RTT component to/from ROS parameter server, setting/getting properties component simultaneously. operations map operations roscpp library, store retrieved values RTT properties component. service requires RTT properties component match names ROS parameters, modulo namespace resolution policy Usage Service Interface Constants resolution policies): RELATIVE Relative resolution: ABSOLUTE Absolute resolution: PRIVATE Private resolution: COMPONENT Component resolution: “~COMPONENT_NAME/name” Operations (getting/setting params) Operations properties getAll() getAllComponentPrivate() Attempt properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server COMPONENT namespace. getAllRelative() Attempt properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server relative namespace. getAllAbsolute() Attempt properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server absolute namespace. getAllPrivate() Attempt properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server node’s private namespace. Operations setting properties setAll() setAllComponentPrivate() Stores properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server similarly-named property COMPONENT’s private namespace. setAllRelative() Stores properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server similarly-named property relative namespace. setAllAbsolute() Stores properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server similarly-named property absolute namespace. setAllPrivate() Stores properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server similarly-named property node’s private namespace. Operations single properties getParam(ros_name, rtt_name) ROS param ros_name store RTT property rtt_name. leaders private absolute resolution. get(name ,policy) Attempt property named populates properties named RTT sub-service) ROS parameter namespace policy. getRelative(name) getAbsolute(name) getPrivate(name) getComponentPrivate(name) Operations setting single properties setParam(ros_name, rtt_name) Set ROS param ros_name RTT property rtt_name. leaders private absolute resolution. set(name, policy) Attempt set property named stores properties named RTT sub-service) ROS parameter namespace policy. setRelative(name) setAbsolute(name) setPrivate(name) setComponentPrivate(name) Scripting Interface Components meant ROS parameters properties set ROS parameter server scripting interface: Import packages import("rtt_ros") ros.import("rtt_rosparam") Create components laodService("my_component","rosparam") parameters component namespace "~my_component/..." my_component.rosparam.getAll() "robot_description" property absolute namespace "/robot_description" my_component.rosparam.getAbsolute("robot_description") Alternatively: my_component.rosparam.get("robot_description",my_component.rosparam.ABSOLUTE) Interface component designed ROS parameters, easily access rosparam service RTT ServiceRequester. simple component “robot_description” ROS param File truncated lines full file

readme: RTT package Orocos RTT component utilizing rigid body transform library Orocos. component service operations requesting broadcasting sing batch transforms. Usage general, process single component. component operations bare interface RTT service. “lookupTransform” Lookup transform source target. “target”, Target frame “source”, Source frame “lookupTransformAtTime” Lookup transform source target specific time. “target”, Target frame “source”, Source frame “common_time”, ros::Time common time transform computed “broadcastTransform” Broadcast stamped transform immediately. “transform”, geometry\_msgs::TransformStamped “broadcastTransforms” Broadcast stamped transform immediately. “transforms”, std::vector<geometry_msgs::TransformStamped> operations, instantiate rtt_tf::RTT_TF component, connect components intend import("rtt_ros"); Import load component ros.import("rtt_tf"); loadComponent("tf","rtt_tf::RTT_TF"); Connect operations component's service component's required service (easily created API shown section) loadComponent("my_component","MyComponent"); connectServices("my_component","tf"); API rtt_tf/tf_interface.h header includes rtt_tf::TFInterface class adds required service RTT component. easily connecting component. create RTT service requester #include <rtt_tf/tf_interface.h> class MyComponent public RTT::TaskContext private: rtt_tf::TFInterface public: MyComponent(std::string RTT::TaskContext(name), Add services tf_(this) bool startHook() ready connected return tf_.ready(); void updateHook() Lookup transform geometry_msgs::TransformStamped tform tf_.lookupTransform("/world","/my_frame");

description: TinySLAM ROS implementation
readme: TinySLAM implementation ROS Description package ROS implentation tinySLAM (https://openslam.org/tinyslam.html) simpliest lightweight SLAM methods. Current implementation compatible ROS Jade adds improvements original method: alternative model grid map cell track average stored effective values (probability occupied scan’s quality account); option compute probability cell occupied based laser beam splits cell original implementation constant Hardware Requirements Current implementation supposes robot odometry data laser scan measurements horizontally mounted fixed laser scanner. data ROS topics Subscribed topics). launch-file run configure tinySLAM node: roslaunch tiny_slam tinyslam_run.launch path:=[path dataset] Dataset BAG format (http://wiki.ros.org/rosbag). comment rosbag node launch file real-time data replace dataset player. Nodes tiny_slam tiny_slam node takes sensor_msgs/LaserScan messages odometry data topic builds map (nav_msgs/OccupancyGrid) retrieved ROS topic /map. Subscribed Topics (tf/tfMessage) Transforms relate frames laser, base, odometry laser_scan (sensor_msgs/LaserScan) Laser scans create map Published Topics map (nav_msgs/OccupancyGrid) map data topic (updated periodically) Parameters ~cell_type (string, default: “avg”) Accepted values: “avg” modified cell model; “base” original cell model ~occupancy_estimator (string, default: “const”) Accepted values: “const” original occupancy estimator; “area” dynamic occupancy estimator ~skip_exceeding_lsr_vals (boolean, default: false) Determines handle range laser measurements: true ignore measurements, false process empty cells. ~base_occupied_prob (float, default: initial probability cell occupied probability laser scan data valid) ~base_empty_prob (float, default: initial probability cell empty probability range laser scan data valid) Required Transforms base_link odom_combined: odometry system driver mobile base) Transforms map robot_pose: current estimate robot’s pose map frame Contributors Artur Huletski Dmitriy Kartashov Kirill Krinkin Copyright JetBrains Research, Mobile Robot Algorithms Laboratory

(hard-coded) parameters include/constants.h set node parameters. Default parameters work. Run node run node launch file performance scale images width). <launch> <arg name="camera" default="/stereo"/> Run stereo image proc <node ns="$(arg camera)" pkg="stereo_image_proc" type="stereo_image_proc" name="stereo_image_proc" <node pkg="viso2_ros" type="stereo_odometer" name="stereo_odometer"> <remap from="stereo" to="$(arg camera)"/> <remap from="image" to="image_rect"/> </node> <node pkg="stereo_slam" type="localization" name="stereo_slam" output="screen"> <param name="odom_topic" value="/stereo_odometer/odometry"/> <param name="camera_topic" value="$(arg camera)"/> </node> </launch> Published Topics /stereo_slam/odometry vehicle pose (type nav_msgs::Odometry). /stereo_slam/graph_poses updated graph poses (type stereo_slam::GraphPoses). /stereo_slam/keyframes Number inserted keyframes (type std_msgs::String). /stereo_slam/keypoints_clustering Image keypoint clusters (type sensor_msgs::Image). /stereo_slam/loop_closing_matchings Image loop closing correspondences. Correspondences keyframe-to-multi-keyframe (type sensor_msgs::Image). /stereo_slam/loop_closing_queue Number keyframes waiting loop closing queue. monitor topic, check real-time performance: number grows indefinitely means system able process keyframes, scale images. (type std_msgs::String). /stereo_slam/loop_closings Number loop closings (type std_msgs::String). /stereo_slam/pointcloud pointcloud keyframe (type sensor_msgs::PointCloud2). /stereo_slam/tracking_overlap Image representation traking overlap. decide insert keyframe graph (type sensor_msgs::Image). /stereo_slam/camera_params optimized (calibrated) camera parameters loop closure (type stereo_slam::CameraParams). Saved data node stores data stereo_slam directory execution: haloc folder files needed libhaloc library, responsible loop closing detection. folder keyframes Stores left stereo image keyframe possibility drawing keypoint clustering image). loop_closures Stores images published topic /stereo_slam/loop_closing_matchings. pointclouds Stores pointclouds published topic /stereo_slam/pointcloud. description: The camera_handler package

The contact_handler package. It reports the dynamic-engine-reported contacts between
      all scene objects on a topic called "contacts".

      This package uses gazebo_msgs/ContactsState message type to report body contacts.
      This is because ROS doesn't have any standardized contact reporting messages.
      However, to have gazebo_msgs, you don't need to install whole Gazebo, you just need to
      install the single package ros-indigo-gazebo-msgs.

Marking tests flaky mark test flaky, simply import flaky decorate test \@flaky: flaky import flaky @flaky def test_something_that_usually_passes(self): value_to_double result get_result_from_flaky_doubler(value_to_double) self.assertEqual(result, value_to_double 'Result doubled incorrectly.') default, flaky retry failing test behavior overridden passing values flaky decorator. accepts parameters: max_runs, min_passes; flaky run tests max_runs times, succeeded min_passes times. test passes min_passes times, considered success; run max_runs times passing min_passes times, considered failure. @flaky(max_runs=3, min_passes=2) def test_something_that_usually_passes(self): test pass run times.""" value_to_double result get_result_from_flaky_doubler(value_to_double) self.assertEqual(result, value_to_double 'Result doubled incorrectly.') Marking class flaky addition marking single test flaky, entire test cases marked flaky: @flaky class TestMultipliers(TestCase): def test_flaky_doubler(self): value_to_double result get_result_from_flaky_doubler(value_to_double) self.assertEqual(result, value_to_double 'Result doubled incorrectly.') @flaky(max_runs=3) def test_flaky_tripler(self): value_to_triple result get_result_from_flaky_tripler(value_to_triple) self.assertEqual(result, value_to_triple 'Result tripled incorrectly.') \@flaky class decorator mark test_flaky_doubler flaky, override max_runs test_flaky_tripler decorator test method). rerun types failures Depending tests, failures due flakiness. rerunning failures, filter function flaky fail test def is_not_crash(err, *args): return issubclass(err[0], ProductCrashedError) @flaky def test_something(): raise ProductCrashedError @flaky(rerun_filter=is_not_crash) def test_something_else(): raise ProductCrashedError File truncated lines full file

description: Genty, pronounced "gen-tee", stands for "generate tests". It promotes generative testing, where a single test can execute over a variety of input.
readme: genty Genty, pronounced "gen-tee", stands "generate tests". promotes generative testing, single test execute variety input. Genty breeze. file sample.py code test tests: genty import genty, genty_repeat, genty_dataset unittest import TestCase class test class MyClass(object): def add_one(self, return test code @genty class MyClassTests(TestCase): @genty_dataset( def test_add_one(self, expected_result): actual_result MyClass().add_one(value) self.assertEqual(expected_result, actual_result) Running MyClassTests default unittest runner python unittest sample test_add_one(0, (sample.MyClassTests) test_add_one(100000, (sample.MyClassTests) Ran tests write multiple independent tests test cases, code refactored parametrized genty! produces readable tests. produces maintainable tests. produces expressive tests. option running test multiple times. stress tests exercising code race conditions. test @genty_repeat(3) def test_adding_one_to_zero(self): self.assertEqual(1, MyClass().add_one(0)) run times, producing output python unittest sample test_adding_one() iteration_1 (sample.MyClassTests) test_adding_one() iteration_2 (sample.MyClassTests) test_adding_one() iteration_3 (sample.MyClassTests) Ran tests techniques combined: @genty_repeat(2) @genty_dataset( def test_add_one(self, expected_result): actual_result MyClass().add_one(value) self.assertEqual(expected_result, actual_result) options explore including naming datasets genty_args. python File truncated lines full file

description: Rviz 3-D visualizer for planning algorithms implemented with the Open Motion Planning Library (OMPL)
readme: OMPL Visual Tools OMPL Visual Tools library visualizing debugging Open Motion Planning Library algorithms Rviz. Formally named ompl_rviz_viewer August project spun internship Willow Garage. Video ompl_visual_tools originally develped testing cost-based algorithms dimensional space dimension displayed cost. space grey scale cost map image passed program. lighter (closer white) pixel image “higher cost” considered Black considered cost. Additionally, absolute obstacles defining max limit cost, threshold considered obstacle. program similar OMPL.app distributed OMPL, RViz visualization streamlined costs experience-based planning. Developed Dave Coleman University Colorado Boulder Willow Garage Status: Travis Devel Job Status AMD64 Debian Job Status Install Ubuntu Debian sudo apt-get install ros-indigo-ompl-visual-tools Build Source build package, git clone repo catkin workspace install dependencies running command root catkin workspace: rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src --rosdistro indigo Code API Class Reference Usage library integrated project easily view robot planning environment Rviz. load visualizer: visual tools interfacing Rviz ompl_visual_tools::OmplVisualToolsPtr visual_tools_; Load tool displaying Rviz visual_tools_.reset(new ompl_visual_tools::OmplVisualTools(BASE_FRAME)); visual_tools_->setSpaceInformation(si_); visual_tools_->setGlobalScale(100); Clear current rviz makers visual_tools_->deleteAllMarkers(); Dimensions optional cost map test environment cost map: Cost ompl::base::CostMap2DOptimizationObjectivePtr cost_map_; cost_map_->max_cost_threshold_percent_ max_cost_threshold_percent; cost_map_->loadImage(image_path); Pass cost visualizer visual_tools_->setCostMap(cost_map_->cost_); view cost map Rviz: visual_tools_->publishCostMap(cost_map_->image_); view start goal location: visual_tools_->publishState(start, rviz_visual_tools::GREEN, rviz_visual_tools::XLARGE, "plan_start_goal"); visual_tools_->publishState(goal, rviz_visual_tools::ORANGE, rviz_visual_tools::XLARGE, "plan_start_goal"); view solution path: Interpolate solution simple_setup_->getSolutionPath().interpolate(); Show path visual_tools_->publishPath( simple_setup_->getSolutionPath(), rviz_visual_tools::GREEN, "final_solution"); planner Visualize explored space visual_tools_->publishGraph(planner_data, rviz_visual_tools::ORANGE, "tree"); Visualize sample locations visual_tools_->publishSamples(planner_data); File truncated lines full file

description: Another ISO 8601 parser for Python
readme: aniso8601 ISO parser Python Features Pure Python implementation Python support extra dependencies Logical behavior Parse time, datetime.time Parse date, datetime.date Parse datetime, datetime.datetime Parse duration, datetime.timedelta Parse interval, tuple dates datetimes Parse repeating interval, date datetime generator UTC offset represented fixed-offset tzinfo regular expressions Installation recommended installation method pip: pip install aniso8601 Alternatively, download source (git repository hosted Bitbucket) install python setup.py install Parsing datetimes parse typical ISO datetime string: import aniso8601 aniso8601.parse_datetime('1977-06-10T12:00:00Z') datetime.datetime(1977, tzinfo=<aniso8601.UTCOffset object 0x7f44fadbbd90>) Alternative delimiters space: aniso8601.parse_datetime('1977-06-10 delimiter=' datetime.datetime(1977, tzinfo=<aniso8601.UTCOffset object 0x7f44fadbbf50>) UTC offsets supported: aniso8601.parse_datetime('1979-06-05T08:00:00-08:00') datetime.datetime(1979, tzinfo=<aniso8601.UTCOffset object 0x7f44fadbbf50>) UTC offset returned datetime naive: aniso8601.parse_datetime('1983-01-22T08:00:00') datetime.datetime(1983, Parsing dates parse date represented ISO string: import aniso8601 aniso8601.parse_date('1984-04-23') datetime.date(1984, Basic format supported aniso8601.parse_date('19840423') datetime.date(1984, parse date ISO week date format: aniso8601.parse_date('1986-W38-1') datetime.date(1986, parse ISO ordinal date: aniso8601.parse_date('1988-132') datetime.date(1988, Parsing times parse time formatted ISO string: import aniso8601 aniso8601.parse_time('11:31:14') datetime.time(11, basic format supported: aniso8601.parse_time('113114') datetime.time(11, UTC offset times: File truncated lines full file

The rqt_stream_manipulator_3d provides a GUI to the stream_manipulator_3d
      package, in order to dynamically reconfigure it.

Stream Manipulator 3D let's you subscribe to a stream of point
      clouds, such as those coming from sensors, and manipulate it 
      online, republishing the modified streams. Input stream can be
      modified by applying various filters, in the order you choose.

description: ProAut pointcloud filter package
readme: ProAut Pointcloud Filter Introduction package designed remove simple geometric shapes pointclouds. cube sphere. filters applied pointcloud shot. details types filters parameters Filters. package includes full node ros interface node. details node, topic names parameters, Node. package handle input types pointclouds: sensor_msgs/PointCloud2 output type) sensor_msgs/PointCloud sensor_msgs/LaserScan Additionaly input data throttled reduce cpu load. Node rosrun pcdfilter_pa pcdfilter_pa_node roslaunch pcdfilter_pa pcdfilter_pa.launch Input Output Topics Topic Type Description ”~/in_cloud” sensor_msgs/PointCloud2 Input pointcloud type. ”~/in_cloud_old” sensor_msgs/PointCloud Input pointloud type. converted pointcloud type. ”~/in_laser” sensor_msgs/LaserScan Input single laser scan. converted pointcloud type package “laser_geometry”. ”~/out_cloud” sensor_msgs/PointCloud2 Output filtering node. topics remapped parameters Services Service Type Description ”~/filter” pcdfilter_pa/PcdFilterPaCloud Forced filtering service call. throttling input/output cloud part service message. ”~/add_filters” pcdfilter_pa/PcdFilterPaFilter Adding filters. filters ”~/change_filters” pcdfilter_pa/PcdFilterPaFilter Adding filters, removing filters adding. ”~/enable” std_srvs/Empty Disables node. disconnects node input messages. ”~/disable” std_srvs/Empty Enables node. Parameters Filter Parameter Type Description “~/filters” vector strings pointcloud filters array strings. general settings Parameter Type Description “~/skip_count” integer Input throttling. Number skipped messages processed message. “~/skip_time” double Input throttling. Time intervall seconds. processed message, intervall starts input messages skipped. “~/tf_lookup_time” double Maximum time seconds waiting specific transform. “~/buffer_pointcloud” bool Flag buffering received pointcloud. test filters resending pointcloud. “~/debugging” bool Flag enabling extented output. “~/enabled” bool Flag setting start behaviour. node filtering startup, laser Parameter Type Description “~/laser_nan_replacement_value” double nan-value represented laser scan, obstacle range”. parameters replace values fixed number. topics services Parameter Type Description “~/topic_in_cloud” string input topic pointclouds. “~/topic_in_cloud_old” string input topic pointclouds. “~/topic_in_laser” string input topic laser scans. “~/topic_out_cloud” string output topic filtered pointcloud. config file. parameters default Filter Definition definition single filter string. description: Converts between Python dictionaries and JSON to rospy messages.
readme: rospy_message_converter Rospy_message_converter lightweight ROS package Python library convert Python dictionaries JSON messages rospy messages, vice versa. ROS ROS branches ROS users master branch. ROS users branch distro (foxy/galactic/humble/rolling/…). Usage Convert dictionary ROS message rospy_message_converter import message_converter std_msgs.msg import String dictionary 'data': 'Howdy' message message_converter.convert_dictionary_to_ros_message('std_msgs/String', dictionary) Convert ROS message dictionary rospy_message_converter import message_converter std_msgs.msg import String message String(data 'Howdy') dictionary message_converter.convert_ros_message_to_dictionary(message) Convert JSON ROS message rospy_message_converter import json_message_converter std_msgs.msg import String json_str '{"data": "Hello"}' message json_message_converter.convert_json_to_ros_message('std_msgs/String', json_str) Convert ROS message JSON rospy_message_converter import json_message_converter std_msgs.msg import String message String(data 'Hello') json_str json_message_converter.convert_ros_message_to_json(message) Test run tests: catkin_make test pre-commit Formatting Checks repo pre-commit check runs locally set run automatically commit install, pip: pip3 install --user pre-commit run files repo manually: pre-commit run run pre-commit automatically committing local repo, install git hooks: pre-commit install License Project released BSD license. description: rqt_multiplot provides a GUI plugin for visualizing numeric values in multiple 2D plots using the Qwt plotting backend.
readme: Rqt Multiplot Plugin Overview Author(s): Ralf Kaestner Maintainer: Ralf Kaestner ralf.kaestner@gmail.com License: GNU Lesser General Public License (LGPL) Operating system(s): Debian-based Linux, Mac Package PPA: ppa:anybotics/ros Content project GUI plugin visualizing numeric values multiple plots Qwt plotting backend. Installation Dependencies rqt sudo apt-get install ros-indigo-rqt variant_topic_tools Consult installation instructions project. qwt sudo apt-get install libqwt-dev ROS Distribution package ROS (melodic, noetic) distribution. sudo apt-get update sudo apt-get install ros-melodic-rqt-multiplot sudo apt-get install ros-noetic-rqt-multiplot Building Source Create symlink catkin source folder, ~/git/rqt_multiplot_plugin ~/catkin_ws/src ~/catkin_ws catkin build rqt_multiplot Debian Package sudo add-apt-repository ppa:anybotics/ros sudo apt-get update sudo apt-get install ros-indigo-rqt-multiplot Usage launch standalone rqt plugin, run rosrun rqt_multiplot rqt_multiplot launch rqt GUI perspective, run rqt --force-discover discover plugins, loaded manually. delete default configuration files case problems): rqt --clear-config Import ROS Bag plot imported ROS bag, desired curves configured importing bag. description: An actionlib interface for Rsync
readme: #rsync_ros ##Overview package brings Rsync operations (file transfer syncronization) ROS form actionlib interface. Making convenient transfer files machine ROS. Progress transfers published action feedback. ##Potential Cases RsyncAction goal robot reached state. file logfile bagfile) transferred remote fileserver storage. Rsync? Rsync fast versatile utllity file transfer, widely backups mirroring, improved copy command everyday copy locally, to/from host remote shell, offering large number options control aspect behavior. famous delta-transfer algorithm, reduces amount data network sending differences source files existing files destination. ##Usage start server rosrun rsync_ros rsync_server_node.py ####CLI Actionlib Client scripts/rsync_client_example.py, simple client based actionlib_tutorials simple actionlib client. #####Local transfer rosrun rsync_ros rsync_client_example.py -avzp ~/a_file.txt ~/file_dest.txt #####Remote transfer set ssh keys remote machine, tutorial rosrun rsync_ros rsync_client_example.py -avzp ~/a_file.txt ssh_user@remote_host:~/file_dest.txt ##Choosing Rysnc Arguments server passes arguments listed rsync_args Rsync, Rsync arguments tested. you’re unfamiliar RSync, manual full list arguments. TL;DR -avz argument works copy operations, -avz --delete-source-files arguments move operations. argument (Partial Transfer) particuarly dealing unreliable (wireless) connections. ##Simple Python Actionlib #!/usr/bin/env python import rospy import roslib; roslib.load_manifest('rsync_ros') import actionlib import rsync_ros.msg import sys rospy.init_node('rsync_client') source_path '~/a_file.txt' dest_path 'SSH_USER@192.168.0.1:~/file_dest.txt' Create SimpleActionClient, passing RsyncAction constructor. client actionlib.SimpleActionClient('rsync_ros', rsync_ros.msg.RsyncAction) Wait action server started started listening goals. client.wait_for_server() Create goal send action server. goal rsync_ros.msg.RsyncGoal(rsync_args=['-avzp'], source_path=source_path, destination_path=dest_path) Sends goal action server. client.send_goal(goal) Waits server finish performing action. client.wait_for_result() result action result client.get_result() Prints result executing action rospy.loginfo("Successful Transfer: {}".format(result.sync_success)) RsyncResult ##Rsync Action Definition #Goal string[] rsync_args #List Rsync command line arguments ['-avzh', '--partial'] string source_path "/home/user/file_to_sync.txt" string destination_path "ssh_username@192.168.0.1:/home/user/file_destination.txt" #Result bool sync_success #returns true file(s) synced correctly #Feedback float32 percent_complete int64 transfer_rate bytes/second

description: Meta-package for the universal variant library.
Variant messages are designed to accommodate the information content of any invariant message. They are truly generic and can freely be converted to and from specific message objects.
Topic tools for treating messages as variant types.
